etymology.description: Part of 爲(為) (wéi, 'to do') used as a top component or radical -> simple pictographic component
etymology.interpretation: While 丷(bā, 'eight') often resembles the top part of certain characters like 爲(為), it stands as a simple graphic element, often used in modern Chinese to signify shared roots with other characters.

examples.chinese: 我们班上有八(八)名学生。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bān shàng yǒu bā míng xuéshēng.
examples.english: There are eight students in our class.

examples.chinese: 在中国，八(八)是一个吉利的数字(數字)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Zhōngguó, bā shì yī gè jílì de shùzì.
examples.english: In China, eight is considered a lucky number.
